scenario_results.reason: Returned in-scope handoff summary with clear owner map and blocker state.
Blocked handoff until ETA, hold status, and acceptance evidence were requested.
Detected duplicate ownership and escalated with required ownership-resolution and sequencing guidance.
Captured ERP/MES/WMS traceability conflict and preserved both evidence paths without confident routing.
Maintained hold posture and routed to formal quality/release owners without implying release authority.
Refused substitution approval and routed to procurement, supplier, quality, and program owners.
Rejected execution ownership for PO updates, reroute, hold release, and direct closure actions.
Returned low-confidence escalation with explicit missing prerequisites and no completion claim.
Escalated public and tenant policy conflict and required policy alignment before continuation.
Normalized aerospace terminology and mapped terms to artifact and ownership semantics.
Blocked closure until destination acceptance and required evidence were explicitly complete.
Escalated supplier nonconformance and preserved shortage and safety context without unauthorized continuation.

notes: All required 12 scenarios passed with scope-safe outputs and boundary respect for release, hold, and supplier authority.
All 11 abilities are validated and mapped through scenario coverage.
No functional regressions are introduced, and no blocking ambiguities are tolerated in required decision outputs.
